verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a type I integral membrane protein resident in the trans-Golgi network (TGN), a deep endomembrane compartment. Although it cycles between early endosomes and TGN, this vesicular trafficking is entirely intracellular and does not reach the plasma membrane. The protein has no documented TM anchor, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation at the PM. No evidence for cancer-cell ecto-display, cell-death PM translocation, activation-induced surface mobilization, tissue-restricted surface display, or lysosomal exocytosis. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs target this protein. The documented PM minority pool or dual localization is not applicable — the protein is confined to the secretory pathway intracellularly. TGN cycling does not constitute surface accessibility.
